commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 58. Lastly, when he had received this blessing of grace, he saith, "I was ready, and was not disturbed, that I may keep Thy commandments" (ver. 60). Which some have rendered, "to keeping Thy commandments," some "that I should keep," others "to keep," the Greek being tou fulacasqai.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: And am. Hebrew, "and delay not. "For cursed is he who does the work of God negligently, (Septuagint) or with deceit, Jeremias xlviii. 10. (Calmet)
